nodes.label: Business case: fast CV tailoring + apply flow
nodes.rationale: Primary goal is making job applications as fast and easy as possible: upload CV, find US jobs filtered by state, rank by fit, tailor CV, export to Word, apply directly.

nodes.label: JSearch poor US/state coverage blocker
nodes.rationale: JSearch API has no reliable US state-level filtering, blocking the core business flow; resolved later by switching to Jooble per the bug backlog note.

nodes.label: Switch to Jooble with US state filtering
nodes.rationale: Resolves the country/location filter bug by replacing JSearch with Jooble, which supports reliable US state-level geo-filtering.

nodes.label: GDPR/privacy backlog: stop persisting client CVs
nodes.rationale: Uploaded CVs persist on disk (services/uploads.js multer storage), tailored CVs in output/, raw CV text in session state — none encrypted/access-controlled/auto-deleted; must be resolved before real client data flows through, and before PayPal payment integration (#19) is built.

nodes.label: Word template options (4-choice picker)
nodes.rationale: Default/Alternate built-in generators, disabled 'Similar to original CV' (blocked on PDF-only readCV()), and an AI-placement engine (docxPlacement.js/planDocxPlacement) that only decides placement, never invents wording, for plain uploaded Word templates vs deterministic docxtemplater merge-tag templates.

nodes.label: Default gapSeverities to major only
nodes.rationale: clientPreferences.gapSeverities currently defaults to all three severities; backlog wants default narrowed to ['major'] only so minor/mild gaps become opt-in.

nodes.label: Unanswered gap-confirmation defaults to skip
nodes.rationale: If user tailors CV without responding to an Accept/Skip/Discuss gap item, treat as skip; the HR/recruiter agent (not a hardcoded rule) should judge per-gap whether silent omission is safe given severity and rationale.

nodes.label: STATUS.md upkeep rule
nodes.rationale: STATUS.md must be rewritten and staged into the same commit as the work that changed state, never a separate 'update status' commit, so the status board stays accurate per package.
